column2: Enable feature employee pikup time auto update option
column4:
"If property FEATURE_EMPLOYEE_TIME_AUTO_UPDATE  is un-checked, there will be no change in employee times when any of the following route modification happens.
1. Employee is removed from route
2. Employee is added to a new route
3. Employee is added to an existing route

If property FEATURE_EMPLOYEE_TIME_AUTO_UPDATE is checked, employee times will be automatically updated based on speed calculated using their previous times and distances.
Note: Time is calculated on the configured shift speed."
column6:
"If property FEATURE_EMPLOYEE_TIME_AUTO_UPDATE  is un-checked, there will be no change in employee times when any of the following route modification happens.
1. Employee is removed from route
2. Employee is added to a new route
3. Employee is added to an existing route

If property FEATURE_EMPLOYEE_TIME_AUTO_UPDATE is checked, employee times will be automatically updated based on speed calculated using their previous times and distances.
Note: Time is calculated on the configured shift speed."

column2: Feature enable shift reject request option in route management
column4: Routing property
column5:
This feature can be used by those who are looking for accept/reject flow in schedule request. This can
also be used in place of adhoc request accept/reject flow.Enable this property to enable the feature.
1. Feature allows to cancel a schedule request.
2. In the routed and custom section on route manage page, an icon dustbin shape is provided to
reject that schedule request.
3. Two additional columns are created Last updated time and Action. In the last updated time columns the time when the schedule request was raised is shown. In the action column a dustbin shape icon is provided to reject the schedule request.
4. The last updated time helps the approver that what time the schedule request was raised and accordingly the approver can take action.
5. When approver rejects the schedule, the schedule get ‘Cancelled’.
6. An email is sent the employee informing that the schedule is cancelled.

column2: Auto deleted the employee will duplicate sign in
column4: Will delete any duplicate sign in of employees(Route which is not signed off by employee)
column5:
At the end of each trip the system will identify all employees in the trip who-
1. Did not board the cab.
2. Travel time and distance of the trip is less than a configured value.

All these cases will be revisited after 6 hours (enough time for all the other trips in which the employee might have
travelled). Till that time all the employees would be in Decision Pending status.

Case 1: No matching trip found

If the employee had not boarded the trip, it will be marked as no show and all no show related communications will
be triggered.

If the employee has signed in and signed out of the trip, it will be marked as Travelled in the trip.

Case 2: Only 1 matching valid trip found

Employee will be marked as travelled in another trip and ETS will store the trip id of such a trip.

Case 3: More than 1 matching valid trips found

Although most of the cases will be resolved in Case 1 and Case 2.

If there are more than 1 matching trips found, all these trips will be flagged in a new billing issue bucket – “Trips
with duplicate employee sign in”

the old SMS format.

column2: can we able to merge the multiple shit schedules
column4: To enable merge schedules option in manage schedules drop down
column5:
If True, on the Route Management page, when router selects multiple shifts enable a button for - Merge Schedules
This option is only applicable if all selected shifts are only Logins or only Logouts.

Show a pop-up and ask user to select the office and shift they want to move all employees to. The shift to which all employees are to be moved may be one of the selected shift or it could be a different shift.

If the employees are moved to a single shift, auto edit employee schedules to the new shift. In the employee shift history logs, show the edit done by Merge Shift By Router Name.

In audit history, capture who has made the edit and when.

Any new schedule coming into these shifts will not be moved automatically. Router will need to move these again to the desired shift.
column6: Allow merging of Two shifts

column2: Configure the last day for schedule expiry notification option
column4:
If an employee, say Emp1 has a schedule for today (7th March), and the property is set to 10 days.
If the employee has a schedule on 18th March (10 days after today), the notification will not be sent.
However, if no schedule is found on this date, the schedule expiry notification is sent to the
employee.

employee.
